items.title: Loss surfaces
items.description: Loss surfaces plot the objective over parameters; convex surfaces are single bowls, while non-convex surfaces show ridges, saddles, and multiple valleys that this topic explains.

items.title: Gradient descent
items.description: Gradient descent follows the slope of f; on convex problems it reliably descends toward the global minimum, while on non-convex problems it may stop at saddles or local minima depending on initialization and step size.

items.title: Jacobian and Hessian
items.description: The Hessian supplies second-order curvature used to test convexity (positive semidefinite) and to classify critical points as minima, maxima, or saddles.

items.title: Multilayer perceptron training
items.description: Training MLP weights minimizes a non-convex loss; understanding convex vs non-convex explains why deep models need careful initialization and learning rates despite sharing the same gradient descent machinery as convex linear models.
